skill.name: algorithmic-art
skill.description: Guides creation of original generative art using p5.js, seeded randomness, reusable templates, and interactive parameter controls.
skill.summary: Create reproducible p5.js generative art with an interactive browser viewer.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found no confirmed malicious behavior or prompt injection. The critical and high static findings are false positives from license prose, art terminology, Markdown code fences, and a JavaScript RegExp exec call; remaining risk is limited to browser-side JavaScript templates and public CDN/font dependencies.

low_findings.title: Public CDN and Font Dependencies
low_findings.description: The viewer template loads p5.js from cdnjs and fonts from Google-hosted domains. This is a real browser network dependency, but I found no evidence that the skill sends credentials, local files, or user data to those services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URLs are directly visible in the HTML template and documentation. The surrounding code only imports libraries and fonts, with no fetch, credential access, or exfiltration logic found.

low_findings.title: Browser-Side JavaScript Template
low_findings.description: The skill includes JavaScript templates for p5.js generative art and asks the assistant to create inline browser artifacts. This is expected for the skill purpose, but users should review generated HTML before sharing or running it in sensitive contexts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain normal p5.js lifecycle and seeded-randomness functions. They run in the browser and do not show shell execution, filesystem access, credential collection, or network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, Windows SAM, and external command findings are not confirmed. They come from Apache license text, art terminology, Markdown code fences, a comment containing particle system text, and RegExp exec used to parse hex colors.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged contexts were reviewed directly and are benign prose, examples, comments, or a RegExp method call. No process execution, cryptographic security logic, Windows credential access, or reconnaissance behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Generative art requests can be hard to turn into reproducible, interactive code. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create seeded p5.js artwork with controls and browser-ready output.
actual_capabilities: Creates an algorithmic art philosophy before implementation.
Guides p5.js sketches with seeded randomness for repeatable results.
Uses a browser viewer template with seed navigation and parameter controls.
Supports sliders, color pickers, reset controls, and image export patterns.
Encourages original generative systems instead of copying existing artists.
Provides a JavaScript reference template for p5.js structure and utilities.
limitations: Generated artwork still needs human review for quality and originality.
The viewer template depends on external p5.js and font CDN URLs.
The skill does not provide a full art engine or automated visual testing.
Large particle systems may need performance tuning on slower devices.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Generative Artwork
use_cases.description: Turn a visual theme into a seeded p5.js sketch with adjustable parameters.

use_cases.title: Build Interactive Art Demos
use_cases.description: Create browser-ready HTML artifacts for presentations, classes, or portfolio experiments.

use_cases.title: Explore Repeatable Variations
use_cases.description: Use seed controls to compare many visual outcomes from one algorithm.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a first generative sketch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an original p5.js generative artwork about wind moving through tall grass. Include a short algorithmic philosophy and an interactive HTML viewer.

prompt_templates.title: Add Tunable Controls
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with specific interface needs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a seeded p5.js artwork with sliders for density, motion speed, turbulence, and trail length. Use the provided viewer template and keep seed navigation working.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Variation System
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an algorithmic art artifact where seeds 1 through 20 create meaningful variations. Explain which parameters change and why each seed remains reproducible.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Conceptual Series
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request combining philosophy, implementation, and polish.
prompt_templates.prompt: Develop an original generative art movement inspired by urban sound patterns. Then implement a polished p5.js viewer with custom controls, color tuning, and PNG export.

output_examples.input: Create generative art inspired by coral reef growth.
output_examples.output: A concise philosophy about branching marine systems, followed by an interactive viewer with seeded growth, color controls, density sliders, and repeatable reef variations.

output_examples.input: Make a flow field artwork with calm and turbulent zones.
output_examples.output: A browser-ready p5.js artifact that exposes seed, particle count, noise scale, speed, and palette controls for exploring controlled motion.

output_examples.input: Generate a series of print candidates from one algorithm.
output_examples.output: A single viewer that uses seed navigation to compare variations and lets the user export selected frames as PNG images.

best_practices: Review generated HTML before running it outside a trusted browser context.
Keep seeded randomness active so each chosen output can be reproduced.
Tune parameters for visual clarity before increasing particle counts or complexity.
anti_patterns: Do not copy a living artist style or request imitation of protected work.
Do not remove seed controls when repeatable output is required.
Do not add unrelated external scripts or tracking code to the viewer.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates original p5.js generative art concepts and interactive HTML viewers.

faq.question: Does it generate static images directly?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on browser-based algorithms that can render and export images.

faq.question: Can the same artwork be reproduced?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill emphasizes seeded randomness so the same seed recreates the same result.

faq.question: Does the viewer need internet access?
faq.answer: The provided template loads p5.js and fonts from public CDN URLs unless those assets are embedded locally.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found no confirmed malicious behavior. The remaining risk is normal browser JavaScript and public CDN loading.

faq.question: Who is this best for?
faq.answer: It is best for artists, designers, educators, and creative coders exploring interactive generative systems.
